title: Expand test suite: supervisor, daemon integration, and remaining coverage gaps
description:
## Context

Initial test suite was added (83 tests, commit 0b87614) covering health, runtime, DNS, scheduler, daemon routes, and plist. Several packages still lack tests.

## Remaining Work

### Supervisor tests (0% coverage, 336 lines)
- Refactor to inject a process factory (instead of direct os/exec) for testability
- Test restart policies: always, on-failure, never
- Test max restarts limit
- Test exponential backoff (5s → 10s → 20s → 60s cap)
- Test Stop (SIGTERM to process group) and Kill (SIGKILL)
- Test StopAll and IsRunning/PID helpers

### Daemon integration tests (Run/startAll/shutdown at 0%)
- Test state file locking (flock-based exclusivity)
- Test clean slate recovery (orphan container cleanup)
- Test signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown)
- Test statsPoller and logPoller goroutines
- Test proxy setup

### Coverage gaps in tested packages
- daemon routes: handleRun (0%), handleStatsByName (33%), handleApply execute path
- scheduler: Start(), runSchedule(), execute() — goroutine-based, need time mocking
- config: validateHealthCheck (29%), more edge cases in validate()
- plist: Install/Uninstall need HOME override or path injection refactor

## Approach

1. Extract `ProcessStarter` interface from supervisor for mock injection
2. Use `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())` for plist file I/O tests
3. Consider `clockwork` or similar for time-dependent scheduler tests
4. Add `-race` flag to CI test command

## Current Coverage Baseline

| Package    | Coverage |
|------------|----------|
| health     | 95.6%    |
| runtime    | 91.7%    |
| dns        | 91.2%    |
| logbuf     | 81.8%    |
| config     | 77.4%    |
| status     | 73.2%    |
| scheduler  | 44.4%    |
| daemon     | 37.6%    |
| plist      | 3.7%     |
| supervisor | 0%       |
